title: Linux Basics — Files, Navigation, Search
html:
Linux Basics — Files, Navigation, Search

Contents

  • Navigation
  • Read / write files
  • Copy / move / delete
  • Find files
  • Search inside files
  • History & shortcuts

Navigation

CommandDescription
 
pwd
print current working directory
cd -
switch to previous directory
cd ~
go to home
pushd /var/log && popd
directory stack — go and return
ls -lah
long listing with hidden + human sizes
ls -lt
newest first
ls -lS
biggest first
tree -L 2
two-level tree view

Read / write files

CommandDescription
 
cat file
dump file
less file
pager (q to quit, / to search)
head -n 50 file
first 50 lines
tail -n 100 file
last 100 lines
tail -f /var/log/syslog
follow log
wc -l file
count lines
touch file
create empty / update mtime
echo 'hi' >  file
overwrite
echo 'hi' >> file
append

Copy / move / delete

CommandDescription
 
cp -av src/ dst/
recursive, preserve perms+timestamps
mv old new
rename
rm -rf dir
recursive delete (DANGEROUS)
ln -s /opt/foo /usr/local/foo
symlink
readlink -f link
resolve symlink to absolute path

Find files

CommandDescription
 
find . -name '*.conf'
by name
find / -type f -size +100M 2>/dev/null
files larger than 100 MB
find . -mtime -1
modified in last 24 h
find . -perm -4000 2>/dev/null
SUID binaries
find . -name '*.log' -delete
find and delete
find . -name '*.py' -exec grep -l TODO {} \;
find + exec grep
locate passwd
use updatedb index (faster than find)
which python3
first match in $PATH
type -a python3
all matches incl. aliases / functions

Search inside files

CommandDescription
 
grep -rni 'password' /etc
recursive, line nr, case-insensitive
grep -E 'foo|bar' file
extended regex
grep -v '^#' /etc/ssh/sshd_config | grep .
non-comment, non-empty lines
grep --include='*.py' -r 'TODO' .
limit by file glob
rg foo
ripgrep (much faster than grep)

History & shortcuts

CommandDescription
 
history | tail -30
recent commands
!42
re-run history entry 42
!!
re-run previous
sudo !!
re-run previous as root
Ctrl-r
interactive reverse history search
Ctrl-l
clear screen (same as clear)
